description: Screens the ACTUAL served content (parametric template instances rendered by templates.instantiate + concrete teach-bank fallback items) against the five held-out ETS practice forms (copyright firewall) and for internal near-duplicates.

within_skill_template.method: one instance per template, compared within each skill (lexical Jaccard k=4, threshold 0.8); exact-duplicate pairs confirmed across seeds [101, 202]+[5001, 5002]

lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_a: Evaluate \( \arcsin\!\left(\sin\dfrac{7\pi}{3}\right) \). Give your answer as a value in \( \left[-\tfrac{\pi}{2},\tfrac{\pi}{2}\right] \).
lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_b: Evaluate \( \arctan\!\left(\tan\dfrac{7\pi}{3}\right) \). Give your answer as a value in \( \left(-\tfrac{\pi}{2},\tfrac{\pi}{2}\right) \).

lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_a: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{9} \) be 9 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \wedge p_2 \) (the truth values of \( p_3, \ldots, p_{9} \) do not appear in the formula). Out of all \( 2^{9} \) truth assignments to \( p_1,\ldots,p_{9} \), how many make \( p_1 \wedge p_2 \) TRUE?
lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_b: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{9} \) be 9 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \rightarrow p_2 \) (the truth values of \( p_3, \ldots, p_{9} \) do not appear in the formula). Out of all \( 2^{9} \) truth assignments to \( p_1,\ldots,p_{9} \), how many make \( p_1 \rightarrow p_2 \) TRUE?

lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_a: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{9} \) be 9 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \wedge p_2 \) (the truth values of \( p_3, \ldots, p_{9} \) do not appear in the formula). Out of all \( 2^{9} \) truth assignments to \( p_1,\ldots,p_{9} \), how many make \( p_1 \wedge p_2 \) TRUE?
lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_b: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{9} \) be 9 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \leftrightarrow p_2 \) (the truth values of \( p_3, \ldots, p_{9} \) do not appear in the formula). Out of all \( 2^{9} \) truth assignments to \( p_1,\ldots,p_{9} \), how many make \( p_1 \leftrightarrow p_2 \) TRUE?

lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_a: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{9} \) be 9 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \rightarrow p_2 \) (the truth values of \( p_3, \ldots, p_{9} \) do not appear in the formula). Out of all \( 2^{9} \) truth assignments to \( p_1,\ldots,p_{9} \), how many make \( p_1 \rightarrow p_2 \) TRUE?
lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_b: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{9} \) be 9 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \leftrightarrow p_2 \) (the truth values of \( p_3, \ldots, p_{9} \) do not appear in the formula). Out of all \( 2^{9} \) truth assignments to \( p_1,\ldots,p_{9} \), how many make \( p_1 \leftrightarrow p_2 \) TRUE?

lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_a: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{21} \) be 21 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \vee \neg p_1 \) (only \( p_1 \) appears in it). Out of the \( 2^{21} \) possible truth assignments to \( p_1, \ldots, p_{21} \), how many make this formula TRUE?
lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_b: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{21} \) be 21 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( p_1 \wedge \neg p_1 \) (only \( p_1 \) appears in it). Out of the \( 2^{21} \) possible truth assignments to \( p_1, \ldots, p_{21} \), how many make this formula TRUE?

lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_a: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{21} \) be 21 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( \bigl(p_1 \wedge (p_1 \rightarrow p_2)\bigr) \rightarrow p_2 \) (only \( p_1, p_2 \) appear in it). Out of the \( 2^{21} \) possible truth assignments to \( p_1, \ldots, p_{21} \), how many make this formula TRUE?
lexical_near_duplicate_candidates.stem_b: Let \( p_1, p_2, \ldots, p_{21} \) be 21 propositional variables, and consider the formula \( (p_1 \leftrightarrow p_2) \vee (p_1 \leftrightarrow \neg p_2) \) (only \( p_1, p_2 \) appear in it). Out of the \( 2^{21} \) possible truth assignments to \( p_1, \ldots, p_{21} \), how many make this formula TRUE?

within_skill_template.note: lexical_near_duplicate_candidates are NOT counted as duplicates: the leakage normalizer strips LaTeX macros, so symbolic-only stems that differ solely by a function/operator macro (e.g. \cos vs \sin, \rightarrow vs \wedge) collapse to identical tokens. Both raw stems are included so the difference is visible. The objective duplicate signal is exact_duplicate_pairs (identical rendered content across seeds).

scope: REFERENCE (copyright) screen: every served item — 5727 parametric template instances (from 2909 templates x up to 2 fixed seeds each) PLUS 246 teach-bank fallback items = 5973 items — was screened for containment (shingle k=4, threshold 0.8) against ALL 5 held-out ETS forms (Practice 1.pdf, Practice 2.pdf, Practice 3.pdf, Practice 4.pdf, Practice 5.pdf). This is comprehensive over the served surface. SELF (near-duplicate) screen: the 246 teach-bank items were all-pairs screened (lexical near-dup is meaningful for concrete prose items). An all-pairs lexical self-screen over all 5727 template instances is intentionally NOT run: it is O(n^2), and parametric instances legitimately share scaffolding while the lexical normalizer deletes LaTeX macros, so it is not a meaningful signal for symbolic stems. Instead a per-skill within-skill template check compares one instance per template: the objective signal is exact_duplicate_pairs (identical rendered stem+choices across seeds); lexical near-dup candidates are recorded with both raw stems for transparency. 'clean' = no ETS leakage AND no teach near-duplicates AND no exact-duplicate templates.
